summary: Deep audit of 12+ service files. Found and fixed 3 real bugs: crash_timeline TypeError from missing simulate_paths args, volatility_analytics mislabeled GARCH model, risk_number silent NaN skipping in portfolio returns.

bugs_found.title: crash_timeline calls simulate_paths without required risk_score and scenario args
bugs_found.description: estimate_crash_timeline() calls simulate_paths() without passing the required risk_score and scenario positional parameters, causing a TypeError at runtime. The API health check shows 'ok' because it only checks import, not execution.
bugs_found.root_cause: simulate_paths signature requires risk_score: float and scenario: dict as positional params with no defaults. crash_timeline was ported from an older version where these params didn't exist.
bugs_found.fix: Added risk_score=risk_score and scenario=<base scenario from config> to the simulate_paths call.

bugs_found.title: GARCH forward curve reports 'GJR-GARCH(1,1) Student-t' but fits standard GARCH(1,1)
bugs_found.description: The _garch_forward_curve function uses arch_model(vol='Garch', p=1, q=1, dist='t') which is standard GARCH(1,1), but the output metadata says 'GJR-GARCH(1,1) Student-t'. GJR-GARCH requires vol='GARCH' with o=1 parameter to capture leverage effects. This produces factually incorrect metadata in all API responses that include volatility analytics.
bugs_found.root_cause: Model label was aspirational (GJR was intended) but the actual arch_model call was never updated to include the o=1 parameter for the asymmetric term.
bugs_found.fix: Changed model label from 'GJR-GARCH(1,1) Student-t' to 'GARCH(1,1) Student-t' to match the actual model being fit.

bugs_found.title: Portfolio returns silently skip NaN via sum(axis=1), deflating returns
bugs_found.description: When computing portfolio returns as (returns * weights).sum(axis=1), pandas sum() with default skipna=True silently skips NaN values. For a 50/50 portfolio where ticker B has NaN, the portfolio return becomes 0.5 * r_A instead of r_A (the correct return when only A has data). This deflates portfolio returns on partial-NaN days, artificially inflating measured volatility and max drawdown, producing an overstated risk number.
bugs_found.root_cause: Default pandas behavior: Series.sum(skipna=True) drops NaN instead of propagating it. The code assumed all tickers would have complete data over the lookback window.
bugs_found.fix: Changed to dropna() on the returns DataFrame BEFORE computing weighted sum, so only rows where all tickers have data are included. This gives correct portfolio returns rather than partially-weighted ones.

false_alarms.suspected_bug: Off-by-one in rolling_mdd: range(window, ...) vs range(window-1, ...)
false_alarms.why_not_a_bug: Returns have one fewer element than prices due to pct_change().dropna(), so rolling(window).mean() on returns starts at prices.index[window], which aligns with range(window, ...) on prices. No off-by-one.

observations: systemic_risk.py absorption ratio threshold (0.85) is almost always triggered because 3 of 6 default assets are highly correlated US equity indices — methodology concern but not a code bug
copula_tail.py has dead code at lines 261-267 (broken stats.gamma.args usage) but it's overwritten by the except block — cosmetic, not a runtime bug
retirement_mc.py drift correction (annual_arith_mu = geometric_mu + 0.5*sigma^2) is mathematically correct despite initially appearing suspicious
